publicationStmt.publisher: "Maryland, Register of Wills Records, 1629-1999." Images. FamilySearch. http://FamilySearch.org: 3 December 2024. Citing Prerogative Court. Hall of Records, Annapolis.

div.head: George Clarke Will
div.p:
In the name of God Amen this thirteenth day of July in the year of
our Lord One Thousand six hundred eighty and three I George Clarke of
Charles County in the provinence of Maryland planter being weake and sick
but of good and perfect memory thanks be to almighty God and --aving
well the uncertaintie of this transitory life do in manner and form ordain
and appoint this to be my last will and testament reworking and
dismantling all former wills and testaments by me heretofore made
-- I give and bequeath my soule to God Almighty disirig the forergiveness and
-- of all my sins hoping through the merits of Christ Jesus my saviour
that at the last day my body with my soule shall rise to eternall life and I also give
my body to be decently buried accoring to the direction of my Executor hereafter
named and as for what personal Estate it hath pleased God Almighty for
above my dire-- to bestowe upon me I do order and disperse of in manner
and form following my Just Debts and Funeral expenses ini the first place being well
and
and truly satisfied and contented.

I-- I do give and bequeath until Richard Hall my god son the son of Richard and Mary
Hall of this county one heifer aged between two and three years of age with her
future increase to have and to hold to him and his heirs forever.

It. I do give and bequeath unto my well beloved friend William Frost one young horse
aged about three years to be delivered within convenient time after my decease

It. I do give and bequeath unto my beloved friend Francis Adams one cow with her
future increase to have and to hold to him and his hiers forever. And after the rest
of my personal Estate that it hath pleased God Almighty to bestowe upon me
I do give anf bequeath unto my well beloved friend John Godshall to have &
to hold to him and his hiers forever anf I do hereby further appoint & ordaine the
In witness whereof I have hereunto set my hand and seal the day and year
first above written

Signed sealed & delivered
In presence of
signed his
John X Wilkenson?-Joseph -- -alph
C-- Lomas

On the back of the foregoing was endorsed the following probate (to will),
August 12th 1684 Came? he Evidences the within written and made oath upon the
holy Evangelist that they saw the within named George Clarke sign seal and
Deliver the within will as his -- and deed and that he was of good dis--
mind at the signing and sealing thereof witness
4 1/4 Sides
Robert Dayne
